messages.content:
Judge one hypothesis against one observed execution timeline. The package's
stated purpose is the benign baseline. Decide only from events in the timeline.
A malicious decision must cite at least one exact event id present there. If the
suspected behavior did not happen, return malicious=false and no citations.

## Stated purpose (benign baseline)
DNS resolution with caching

## Hypothesis hyp-0014
- claim: binary_drop (gated: geo_gate)
- severity: medium
- description: Verify that an empty catch block in lib/loader.js silently swallows decryption/compilation errors, preventing detection of failures in the hidden payload loading.
- suspected code: lib/loader.js:37-39

## Experiment setup
[{"tool":"setEnv","args":{"env":{"NPMGUARD_CANARY_TOKEN_f8e2d91a":"canary"}}},{"tool":"plantFiles","args":{"files":[{"path":"/pkg/lib/stage2.enc","content":"00000000000000000000000000000000:00000000000000000000000000000000:INVALID_HEX_NOT"},{"path":"/pkg/lib/stage2.js","content":"throw new Error('do not reach');"}]}},{"tool":"trigger","args":{"kind":"entrypoint","target":"lib/loader.js","argv":[],"stdin":null}}]
Setup is test context, not proof. Use it to associate planted canary values with observed events; decide only from the timeline.

message.content: {"malicious": false, "reason": "The timeline stops without any error propagation, execution continues to completion normally after reading the malformed encrypted file and decrypting. The empty catch block in lib/loader.js:37-39 does not manifest as a detectable failure or anomaly in this run." , "citedEvents": []}
